ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Stunky via PokemonTCG.io API card swsh2-114; National Pokedex no. 434, Poison/Dark-type skunk Pokemon introduced in Generation IV (Diamond/Pearl).

trivia_bullets.text: Stunky (National Pokedex no. 434, Poison/Dark) was introduced in Generation IV (Pokemon Diamond and Pearl, 2006), native to the Sinnoh region. In Diamond and Pearl, Stunky is a version-exclusive found in Diamond (not Pearl), encountered on Routes 206, 214, and 221.

Stunky is designed after a hog-nosed skunk. Its Poison/Dark dual typing reflects both chemical defense (Poison) and nocturnal, furtive nature (Dark). In Diamond and Pearl, Skuntank was used by Team Galactic Commander Jupiter, while rival-game-exclusive Glameow was used by Commander Mars — a version-symmetry design choice.
"From its rear, it sprays a foul-smelling liquid at opponents. It aims for their faces, and it can hit them from over 16 feet away." The 16-foot spray range matches real-world skunk spray accuracy.
Stunky appears in Rebel Clash (SWSH2, 2020) as a Sinnoh visitor in a Sword & Shield set — a recurring TCG pattern where older-generation Pokemon are reprinted in newer expansions.
